title: Pan-Seared Punjabi Dumplings
description: A fusion of Indian and Chinese flavors, these pan-seared dumplings are a hearty and satisfying meal. Serve with your favorite chutney or sauce.
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, water, and punjabi spice blend. Mix until a dough forms.
2. Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
3. Divide the dough into 20-24 equal portions.
4. Roll out each portion into a thin circle.
5. Place a wonton wrapper in the center of each dough circle.
6. Add a small spoonful of the onion-garam masala mixture to the center of each wonton wrapper.
7. Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle or a square shape.
8. Press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
9. Heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or oil in a large non-stick pan over medium heat.
10. Add the dumplings to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
11. Serve the dumplings hot with your favorite chutney or sauce.
